hiragana.mnemonic:
あ (a): Think of an Apple with a stem.
い (i): Imagine two "i"s side-by-side, like two eyes.
う (u): A boxer getting punched in the stomach, saying "Ugh!"
え (e): An Energetic ninja running.
お (o): A UFO in the sky. Note the extra stroke compared to "a".

か (ka): A blade cutting a stick.
き (ki): It looks like a key.
く (ku): A cuckoo bird’s beak.
け (ke): A cake.
こ (ko): Two koi fish swimming around each other.

Dakuon: が (ga), ぎ (gi), ぐ (gu), げ (ge), ご (go)

さ (sa): A sad face (missing one line).
し (shi): A fishing hook.
す (su): A sip of water (or straw in a glass).
せ (se): A mouth about to say something.
そ (so): A zigzag sewing stitch.

Dakuon: ざ (za), じ (ji), ず (zu), ぜ (ze), ぞ (zo)

た (ta): Looks like a dog.
ち (chi): A cheerleader (mirror of さ).
つ (tsu): A tsunami wave.
て (te): Looks like a hand.
と (to): A tornado.

Dakuon: だ (da), ぢ (ji), づ (zu), で (de), ど (do)

な (na): A complicated knot (or nut).
に (ni): A knee.
ぬ (nu): Chopsticks holding noodles.
ね (ne): A snail behind a nail.
の (no): A "no" sign (circle).

は (ha): Looks like an H with a loop.
ひ (hi): A smiling face saying "he he".
ふ (fu): Mount Fuji.
へ (he): An arrow pointing up to the Heaven.
ほ (ho): A horse’s face.

Dakuon: ば (ba), び (bi), ぶ (bu), べ (be), ぼ (bo)
Handakuon: ぱ (pa), ぴ (pi), ぷ (pu), ぺ (pe), ぽ (po)

ま (ma): A man wearing a mask.
み (mi): A musical note.
む (mu): A cow saying "moo".
め (me): A messy face (dropped noodles).
も (mo): A monitor lizard with a long tail.

や (ya): A yak with horns.
ゆ (yu): A unicorn.
よ (yo): A yo-yo.

ら (ra): A rabbit sitting.
り (ri): Two lines like a river flow.
る (ru): A hand holding a ruby.
れ (re): A reindeer looking up.
ろ (ro): Something got robbed (missing ruby).

わ (wa): A white swan.
を (wo / o): A crack in the wall.
ん (n): Looks like a simple "n".

katakana.mnemonic:
ア (a): Looks like an axe.
イ (i): Looks like an easel on its side.
ウ (u): Angular version of う (hiragana).
エ (e): Elevator doors.
オ (o): An opera singer.

カ (ka): Angular version of か.
キ (ki): Like き but simpler (no bottom part).
ク (ku): A cuckoo’s tail.
ケ (ke): Looks like the letter K.
コ (ko): A road with two corners.

Dakuon: ガ (ga), ギ (gi), グ (gu), ゲ (ge), ゴ (go)

サ (sa): A saddle.
シ (shi): A sinking ship.
ス (su): A person skiing.
セ (se): Like せ but simpler.
ソ (so): A soft-serve ice cream.

Dakuon: ザ (za), ジ (ji), ズ (zu), ゼ (ze), ゾ (zo)

タ (ta): A person holding a tablet.
チ (chi): A chicken.
ツ (tsu): A tuna fish head.
テ (te): A bent telephone pole.
ト (to): A totem pole (side view).

Dakuon: ダ (da), ヂ (ji), ヅ (zu), デ (de), ド (do)

ナ (na): A curved knife.
ニ (ni): Straight version of に.
ヌ (nu): A Noose.
ネ (ne): A nest in a tree.
ノ (no): A simple “no” sign.

ハ (ha): A roof of a house.
ヒ (hi): The side of a heel.
フ (fu): The tip of a foot.
ヘ (he): Same as hiragana へ.
ホ (ho): A shining cross.

Dakuon: バ (ba), ビ (bi), ブ (bu), ベ (be), ボ (bo)
Handakuon: パ (pa), ピ (pi), プ (pu), ペ (pe), ポ (po)

マ (ma): A manta ray.
ミ (mi): Look for the middle line.
ム (mu): Moose antlers.
メ (me): A box = “mail”.
モ (mo): Like も but lines don’t cross.

ヤ (ya): Angular version of や.
ユ (yu): U-boat's periscope .
ヨ (yo): Something pulled by oxen.

ラ (ra): A lounge chair.
リ (ri): Simplified り.
ル (ru): Roots of a tree.
レ (re): A razor blade.
ロ (ro): A robot’s mouth.

ワ (wa): A wine glass.
ヲ (wo / o): Part of an Olympic torch.
ン (n): A spacecraft entering atmosphere.
